rationale: Both events (A0, EV-001) show spoolsv.exe loading legitimate, validly signed Microsoft DLLs (PrintConfig.dll and mxdwdrv.dll) from the expected printer driver path C:\Windows\System32\spool\drivers\x64\3\. Both DLLs carry valid Microsoft Windows signatures, correct original file names, and version strings consistent with Windows 10 build 19041. This is normal Print Spooler behavior when a print driver is initialized. No unsigned, unexpected, or out-of-path DLLs are present, and no process creation, network, or registry events accompany the loads to suggest exploitation (e.g., PrintNightmare-style abuse would typically involve a driver dropped from a UNC path or a non-standard directory).
